mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports valuation multiples, so 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive when policy is easing relative to the prior month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with a month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals worsening labor demand and weaker household income growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal effect disappears after controlling for the prior 3-month UNRATE trend.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS implies resilient consumer demand and better near-term revenue expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth does not coincide with above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect flips in more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in INDPRO and HOUST indicates improving real activity and capacity utilization, which should support equity returns over the next quarter through stronger earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal from INDPRO and HOUST is not stronger than either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of joint-upturn observations.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 accelerates above its recent trend, it signals stronger aggregate demand and higher expected earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth exceeds its prior 2-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDPC1 readings fail to predict positive 60-day returns, or if the conditional mean return is not higher than the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL inflation combined with low FEDFUNDS implies real rates are still restrictive; if inflation remains elevated while policy does not tighten further, equity returns over the next month should be negative as multiple compression dominates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rises while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
regime_hint: disinflation lag or policy lag regime
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL inflation and FEDFUNDS is insignificant, or if triggered episodes do not show lower 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS series indicates payroll gains that support consumption and earnings revisions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next week when payroll momentum is improving.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month PAYEMS gains fail to predict positive 5-day returns, or if the sign becomes negative in a majority of rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized slowdown in INDPRO and HOUST signals broad real-activity deterioration and weaker cyclical earnings momentum, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for 2 consecutive months and HOUST declines on the latest reading
failure_condition: Reject if joint declines in INDPRO and HOUST are not followed by below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction adds no predictive value beyond either proxy alone.
